name: R1 -- C10 Single-Source-of-Truth Consistency Check + Fixtures (validate.sh 9->10)

design_notes.three_mode_pinning: tools/validate.sh remains the 3-MODE script contract 026 pinned (default / --target <file> / --payload). This contract pins C10's behavior in EACH mode. (1) DEFAULT -- sweeps the SAME set C9 sweeps: build_shipped_md_set()'s output UNION {docs/guide/index.html} (the `c9_default_files` array C9 already builds in main(); C10 is registered immediately after C9 and reuses that array rather than rebuilding it). (2) --TARGET -- for a `*.md` target: after check_c9_path_resolution, add `check_c10_consistency "${target}"` (exactly that one file). For a `*.html` target: UNLIKE C9 (which contract 026 deliberately did not extend to a `*.html` case), C10 DOES require a `*.html` --target case, because feature_list_004 F-027 acceptance test 6 (`bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/version_drift.html` must FAIL) has no other reachable path -- the version-anchor sub-check's only current real-world target is docs/guide/index.html, an .html file, and C9's target switch has no `*.html)` case to reuse. This contract therefore ADDS a new `*.html)` case to the --target switch that runs ONLY `check_c10_consistency "${target}"` (no C1/C2/C3/C9 -- those checks are not defined for, or not required over, a standalone html target; adding them would be untested scope creep matching contract 026's own restraint). (3) --PAYLOAD -- after check_c9_path_resolution's payload-mode call, add `check_c10_consistency "${payload_md[@]}"` (the payload doc set only, built by build_payload_md_set -- NOT docs/guide/index.html). Justification for including C10 in --payload (all three sub-checks, not skipping the check itself): sub-check (1) payload-set-consistency and sub-check (2) discovery-order both have real targets inside the payload set (standard/*.md including GIP.md's drift-detection section; tools/interface.md's discovery order) and every other content check (C1,C2,C3,C5,C7,C9) already runs in --payload against this same doc set -- omitting C10 wholesale would be an unexplained, inconsistent carve-out (the same reasoning contract 026 gave for including C9 in --payload). Sub-check (3) framework-version-anchor is NOT separately gated or special-cased in --payload: it is written generically (scan for a `framework-version` meta/footer anchor pattern in whatever file is being checked; compare against NIZAM.json if and only if a match is found) so that in --payload mode it naturally, non-vacuously finds ZERO anchors (docs/guide/index.html -- the only file embedding this anchor -- is never in the payload set, since bootstrap.sh does not inject it) and therefore passes trivially, exactly like C7's per-mode module list or C5's per-mode extra-targets list already do for their own mode-conditional inputs; this is a designed pass-through, not a suppressed check. Verified non-breaking on the current tree (post the three content fixes below) in ALL three modes by dry-run at contract-authoring time: default SUMMARY 10/0, payload-mode SUMMARY 9/0, both target cases (*.md and the new *.html) behave as pinned. print_usage()'s 'Checks' block, the 'Modes:' section (documenting the new `*.html` --target case), and the total counts are all updated.
design_notes.ndebt_003_decision:
EXPLICIT DECISION (per the orchestrator's framing, choosing option (i)): FIX both NDEBT-003 items now, as minimal, narrowly-scoped in-scope content fixes -- mirroring the F-026 precedent (which fixed two real pre-existing broken refs, required for its own AT2 non-breaking gate, via the identical narrow-scope-expansion discipline) -- rather than deferring them. Rationale: (a) docs/planning/DEBT.md's own NDEBT-003 remediation column already commits 'Address in F-027 ... fix the guide key-docs list and the line-72 bare ref'; deferring would contradict the debt register's own recorded plan without a new justification. (b) Neither fix is a general doc-freshness pass -- each is a single, precisely-located, objectively-described correction (one enumeration-completeness addition; one bare stale filename token swap), the same size/shape/risk class as contract 026's two fixes. (c) Both fixes are CONTENT corrections, not new automated general-purpose checks -- see 'no_fourth_subcheck_decision' below for why C10 does NOT gain a fourth, generalized 'recurrence guard' sub-check to mechanically catch this class going forward. Concretely: (i) docs/guide/index.html's methodology 'Key documents' list (a `

...

` block presently enumerating `00_planning.md` through `04_tool_driven_state.md` and `06_release_train.md`, silently skipping the new `05_eval_and_trace.md` methodology/06_release_train.md's own v0.5.x renumbering inserted) gains one additional `methodology/05_eval_and_trace.md` list entry, in numeric position between the existing `04_tool_driven_state.md` and `06_release_train.md` entries -- no other content in that paragraph changes. (ii) methodology/06_release_train.md Section 3.2's Minor-change example sentence -- `(as this phase's \`00_planning.md\` through \`05_release_train.md\` were, at initial framework genesis)` -- has its bare, non-'/'-qualified, now-nonexistent filename token `05_release_train.md` corrected to `06_release_train.md` (the file's real current name), the exact same fix-pattern contract 026 already applied to tools/interface.md line 46 and docs/guide/index.html line 535 for the identical root-cause drift (main's v0.5.x methodology renumbering inserting a new slot-05 doc and shifting the release-train protocol doc from slot 05 to slot 06). Per NDS Sec 4 / validator C8 discipline, methodology/06_release_train.md's frontmatter `version` bumps 0.2.0 -> 0.3.0 with one new `change_log` entry naming this exact fix; docs/guide/index.html carries no frontmatter and is not in the C1/C2/C3/C8 shipped-doc set, so its edit carries no version-bump obligation (identical reasoning to contract 026's guide edit).
design_notes.no_fourth_subcheck_decision: C10 gains exactly the three sub-checks the spec names (payload-set, discovery-order, version-anchor) -- NO fourth 'enumeration-completeness' or 'bare-cross-ref' sub-check is added, even though docs/planning/DEBT.md's NDEBT-003 remediation text says C10 should 'guard against recurrence.' Rationale: neither the guide's key-documents enumeration-completeness gap nor a bare (non-'/'-qualified) stale filename token is naturally covered by any of the three spec-named sub-checks (payload-set consistency is specifically about the standard/+templates/+schema/+tools/ 4-directory payload enumeration, not an arbitrary methodology-doc list; discovery-order is specifically about the .nizam/-vs-repo-root skill.json sequence; version-anchor is specifically about the framework-version meta/footer value) -- inventing a general-purpose 'any enumeration must be complete' or 'any bare stale filename token is forbidden' checker robust enough to avoid false-fails (e.g. on legitimate historical narration, cross-repo terms, or same-directory markdown-link targets -- the exact classes C9's own '/'-qualification requirement was added to exclude) is real, un-spec'd, un-scoped design work that risks exactly the false-positive/false-negative classes this phase's own R3 verification-authoring standard (methodology/02 Sec 10) exists to prevent shipping without dedicated fixture coverage. Landing it here would be scope creep beyond 'C10 generalizes three proven one-offs' (spec Sec 5, F-027). Recurrence prevention for this specific class is therefore explicitly DEFERRED, logged as a new follow-up debt item (NDEBT-005, to be added to docs/planning/DEBT.md by the orchestrator after this feature lands, per the WI-1/WI-2/026 precedent of the generator not touching DEBT.md directly) rather than silently built into C10.
design_notes.c10_subcheck_design_and_normalization_fi:
check_c10_consistency [ ...] composes the three sub-checks per swept file. PREPROCESSING (shared by all three sub-checks): strip a leading YAML frontmatter block (identical NR==1/=='---' / matching-'---' awk logic already inline in check_c9_path_resolution; duplicated here rather than extracted into a shared helper, a deliberate strictly-additive choice that leaves check_c9_path_resolution's existing code untouched) into a per-file BODY temp file. This is CONFIRMED LOAD-BEARING, precisely stated: tools/interface.md's own change_log line 11 contains the EXACT literal phrase 'framework-checkout fallback' (a true substring match, describing the H4 fix itself) but does NOT contain the exact literal phrase 'bootstrapped-consumer discovery' (broken by the intervening text '.nizam/tools/skill.json'); without frontmatter-stripping, sub-check (2)'s unstripped first-match line numbers would be fallback_line=11 (frontmatter) vs boot_line=34 (body) -- boot_line > fallback_line -- which would FALSELY FAIL tools/interface.md against ITSELF. Frontmatter-stripping is therefore not merely tidy but strictly required for tools/interface.md to pass its own sub-check (2). REVISION HISTORY (three line/physical-line-based join designs were rejected in succession; this is the REDESIGNED, human-mandated, format-agnostic mechanism that replaces ALL of them -- none of the prior line-join designs may be reimplemented): REV0 (rejected; .agent/qa/027-contract-review.json revision_reviewed=0) joined the ENTIRE blank-line-delimited paragraph before calling vlib_no_stale_payload. FALSE-PASS: a stale 3-directory enumeration sharing one paragraph with an unrelated, legitimate `tools/`-mentioning sentence got merged, the merged blob then contained all four directory tokens, and `[C10] PASS` was wrongly reported. REV1 (rejected; revision_reviewed=1) narrowed the join to a sentence-terminal-OR-3-line-cap window (closing at `. ; : ! ?` line-endings, OR after 2 continuation lines, OR at a blank line). This closed the rev0 false-pass, but introduced its OWN two false-NEGATIVES: (a) the hard 3-physical-line cap silently fragmented a genuine stale 3-directory enumeration spanning MORE than 3 physical lines before its own terminal punctuation (an unpunctuated 4-line prose wrap and a realistic 4-line bulleted list both silently PASSed genuinely stale content); (b) treating a line-final `;`/`:` as an unconditional terminator force-closed a group mid-enumeration when a stale enumeration's only line-break fell at a `;`/`:` list separator rather than a true sentence end (silently PASSed). REV2 (rejected; the immediately-prior revision of THIS contract) removed the hard cap and excluded `;`/`:` as terminators, closing a group ONLY at a physical-LINE-final `.`/`!`/`?` (optionally followed by a closing quote/paren/emphasis char) or a blank line. This correctly closed BOTH rev1 false-negatives and re-confirmed the rev0 false-pass fix -- but is STILL A PHYSICAL-LINE-GRANULARITY mechanism, and the redesign mandate identified its remaining, structural weakness: any doc format (chiefly HTML) where two independent sentences are separated only by inline markup on ONE physical line -- e.g. `

...only.

Elsewhere...tools/...

` all on a single source line, which real authoring tools and hand-edits both plausibly produce -- is NEVER split by ANY physical-line-boundary-based close condition, because the close test only ever fires at a physical line's END, and here the sentence boundary falls MID-LINE. This is not a boundary-rule bug fixable by further patching (another cap value, another terminator set) -- it is a category error: correctness cannot depend on where authors happen to insert line breaks. REV3 -- THE REDESIGN (THIS REVISION, human-authorized attempt 4, format-agnostic by construction, replacing the line-join mechanism ENTIRELY): sub-check (1) is now a three-stage NORMALIZE -> SEGMENT -> CHECK pipeline that never inspects physical line boundaries at all. STAGE 1 (NORMALIZE, via a single python3 invocation -- python3 is already a hard, fail-closed dependency of tools/validate.sh, so this introduces no new runtime dependency): read the frontmatter-stripped body's full text; strip every HTML/XML-style tag span (`<[^>]*>`, replaced with a single SPACE, not removed entirely -- replacing with a space rather than deleting prevents two words that were only tag-separated, e.g. `the hosts`, from being wrongly concatenated into one token; this is what makes `tools/` normalize to ` tools/ ` and what makes a `

` boundary normalize to a plain space between the two sentences, regardless of whether they shared one physical source line); then collapse EVERY run of whitespace (including all newlines) to a single space and strip leading/trailing whitespace -- this is what makes physical line-wrapping completely irrelevant: a 5-line wrapped sentence and a 1-line sentence normalize to the identical single-line text shape. STAGE 2 (SEGMENT): split the normalized single-line text into sentence segments using the regex lookbehind pattern `(?<=[.!?])\s+` (Python `re.split`) -- a segment boundary exists ONLY immediately after a `.`/`!`/`?` that is followed by one or more whitespace characters (or end-of-stream, which `re.split` handles naturally since a trailing unmatched tail is still returned as the final segment). This is deliberately narrower than any line-based rule: (a) it NEVER splits on `;`/`:` (they are not in the character class at all, unconditionally resolving rev1's false-negative (b) by construction, not by a boundary-tuning exception); (b) it NEVER splits inside a filename/token like `standard/GIP.md` or `tools/verify_lib.sh` -- the `.` there is immediately followed by a letter, not whitespace, so the lookbehind's required `\s+` never matches there, leaving the token intact; (c) it splits a >3-line (or any-length) wrapped sentence at its own real terminal period regardless of how many original physical lines it spanned, resolving rev1's false-negative (a) by construction (there is no line count anywhere in this stage); (d) because HTML tags were already replaced by spaces in Stage 1, a stale `

...only.

` immediately followed on the SAME physical line by an unrelated `

...tools/...

` normalizes to '...only. Elsewhere...tools/...' -- the `.` is now immediately followed by whitespace (where the tag used to be), so segmentation correctly splits there regardless of original line layout, resolving the rev2 HTML-collapsed-`

` false-pass by construction, not by adding an HTML-specific carve-out. Blank lines and paragraph breaks need no special handling in this design (they are absorbed by Stage 1's whitespace collapse, then segmentation still finds the real sentence boundaries within). STAGE 3 (CHECK): each non-empty segment (stripped of leading/trailing whitespace) is written as its own line into a temp file, and `vlib_no_stale_payload` is invoked EXACTLY ONCE against that file (composing the vetted, unmodified F-023 primitive -- unchanged compositional discipline across all four attempts; tools/verify_lib.sh is never modified). PIPEFAIL SAFETY (a structural benefit of the redesign, verified by dry run): unlike a grep-driven line-extraction pipeline, this normalize/segment stage is implemented as ONE python3 invocation per file that reads a file argument and always exits 0 on ordinary input (including an empty or already-clean file) -- there is no `grep`/`head`/`cut` pipeline in this stage whose legitimate 'no match' exit code could trip `set -euo pipefail`, so sub-check (1) carries no version of the pipefail hazard sub-checks (2)/(3) still must guard against (see below); this was verified directly by feeding an empty file through the full frontmatter-strip -> normalize -> segment pipeline under `set -euo pipefail` and confirming no abort. VERIFIED BY DRY RUN (redesign, executed against the real repository and every fixture, not merely reasoned about): (i) standard/GIP.md's genuine Section 4 item 3 defect (unfixed) is caught -- see pre_existing_finding_gip for the exact resulting segment; (ii) docs/architecture/ADR-001-ci-compliance-enforcement.md's multi-line-wrapped 4-dir enumeration normalizes and segments to one sentence containing all four tokens, correctly PASSing; (iii) docs/guide/index.html's `

`-wrapped enumeration and its ASCII directory-tree `

` block (tag-stripped and whitespace-collapsed like everything else; the tree's box-drawing characters are not HTML tags and are unaffected by tag-stripping, and the resulting segment legitimately contains `standard/`,`templates/`,`schema/`,`tools/` together) both correctly PASS -- confirmed directly against the real file, not a synthetic proxy; (iv) the rev0 adversarial probe (a co-located, unrelated `tools/` sentence in the same paragraph) is still correctly REJECTED-only-on-the-stale-segment: sentence-segmentation on `[.!?]` alone, independent of any HTML or line structure, was always what closed this defect; (v) the rev1 false-negative fixtures (a >3-line unpunctuated wrap; a `;`-broken enumeration) are both correctly CAUGHT under the redesign (segmentation reconstructs each across however many original lines, and never splits on `;`); (vi) a NEW guide-styled HTML fixture reproducing the rev2 defect -- a stale `
...only.
` immediately followed, NO blank line, by an unrelated `
...tools/...
`, both tags on the boundary between the two sentences -- is now correctly CAUGHT (tag-stripping plus whitespace-collapse exposes the `.`+whitespace boundary Stage 2 needs, independent of original physical-line layout). Full-corpus re-run (default-mode's 38 files union the guide, and payload-mode's 21 files) with this redesigned pipeline: WITHOUT the standard/GIP.md fix, exactly one failure (standard/GIP.md itself) in default mode; WITH all four content fixes applied, ZERO remaining sub-check-(1) failures in BOTH default and payload mode -- matching the required non-breaking-and-forced claim exactly. IMPLEMENTATION HAZARD (unchanged, still load-bearing for sub-checks 2/3 -- NOT applicable to the redesigned sub-check 1, see PIPEFAIL SAFETY above): tools/validate.sh runs under `set -euo pipefail`. Sub-checks (2) and (3) below extract a first-matching line number or captured value via `grep ... | head -1 | cut ...` / `grep -oE ... | head -1`; grep legitimately returns nonzero when a pattern is simply ABSENT (the common, expected case for files with no discovery-order labels or no version anchor at all), and under `pipefail` a bare assignment of such a pipeline would abort the entire script. Every such extraction MUST be written defensively (e.g. `boot_line=$(grep -inE '...' "${body_tmp}" | head -1 | cut -d: -f1) || true` immediately followed by an explicit `[ -n "${boot_line}" ]` presence test before use, or an equivalent `if line=$(...); then ... fi` form) so an absent match is treated as 'sub-check not applicable to this file', never as a script-aborting error -- verified by dry run that this exact hazard is real (grep exits 1 on every file lacking a discovery-order label or version anchor, which is most of the swept corpus). SUB-CHECK (1) payload-set consistency: normalize + segment the body (above) and call `vlib_no_stale_payload` once against the per-segment temp file; a nonzero return is a FAIL, reported as `: [payload-set] `. SUB-CHECK (2) discovery-order (P3-scoped, UNCHANGED across all revisions/the redesign): on the (non-normalized) frontmatter-stripped body, defensively extract the first line number matching the case-insensitive literal phrase 'bootstrapped-consumer discovery' (`boot_line`) and the first line number matching the case-insensitive literal phrase 'framework-checkout fallback' (`fallback_line`) -- both phrases are the exact canonical structural labels tools/interface.md Section 2 items 1/2 and docs/guide/index.html's mirrored discovery list both use verbatim (verified: no other shipped doc, including CONTEXT.md, contains either phrase -- CONTEXT.md's own discovery-adjacent sentence, 'An agent resolves NIZAM.json first, then tools/skill.json', uses neither canonical label, so it is naturally, non-vacuously exempt from this sub-check by CONTENT, not by a CONTEXT.md-specific carve-out, satisfying P3 without any file-identity special-casing). The sub-check applies ONLY when BOTH labels are present in the same file (absence of either means the file does not describe the bootstrapped-consumer-vs-fallback sequence at all, so the sub-check is vacuously not-applicable, not a pass-by-default over unrelated content); when both are present, FAIL if `boot_line` > `fallback_line`, reported as `: [discovery-order] 'Framework-checkout fallback' (line ) precedes 'Bootstrapped-consumer discovery' (line )`. SUB-CHECK (3) framework-version anchor (External Anchor Rule, UNCHANGED across all revisions/the redesign): read `expected_version` ONCE per check_c10_consistency invocation via `python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('NIZAM.json'))['framework']['version'])"` (the expected value is READ from NIZAM.json, NEVER re-derived from the doc under test -- honoring the External Anchor Rule verbatim). For each file's frontmatter-stripped body, defensively extract (if present) a `` value and a `X.Y.Z` value; for each that IS present and differs from `expected_version`, FAIL, reported as `: [version-anchor]  framework-version 'X.Y.Z' != NIZAM.json framework.version ''`. A file with neither anchor present (every file except docs/guide/index.html today) is not-applicable for this sub-check, exactly as designed for --payload mode (design_notes.three_mode_pinning). All per-file temp files are removed with an explicit `rm -f` at the end of each file's iteration (matching check_c8_version_changelog's existing head_tmp convention, not a function-wide trap). `[C10] PASS consistency` / `[C10] FAIL consistency` (with each offending sub-check's tagged detail line indented beneath) is emitted exactly once per check_c10_consistency invocation, mirroring every other check function's single-summary-line convention.
design_notes.pre_existing_finding_gip: CONTRACT-AUTHORING-TIME DISCOVERY (AH-2, confirmed present via the dry run described above, NOT previously logged in docs/planning/DEBT.md -- a genuinely new finding surfaced only once a preprocessing mechanism was designed to fix the ADR-001/guide false-fails, and RE-CONFIRMED unchanged across every revision including the REV3 normalize/segment redesign): standard/GIP.md Section 4 ('Drift Detection'), numbered item 3 ('Detecting local mutation'), reads 'If an injected file under `standard/`, `templates/`, or `schema/` no longer matches the content shipped at the recorded pinned tag, the consumer has drifted through local mutation' -- omitting `tools/` from the list of injected, and therefore equally mutation-detectable, directories. ACCURATE CLOSE-MECHANISM (corrected per the Mode A validator's non-blocking rev1 observation, restated for the REV3 redesign since neither a line cap nor a physical-line boundary is involved any more): this item contains no HTML tags to strip, so Stage 1 only collapses its two physical lines into one normalized run of text; Stage 2 then finds exactly one segment boundary -- immediately after the `.` in '...consumer has drifted through local mutation.', which is followed by whitespace (the next sentence, item 4, 'Detecting staleness.'). The resulting segment is 'If an injected file under `standard/`, `templates/`, or `schema/` no longer matches the content shipped at the recorded pinned tag, the consumer has drifted through local mutation.' -- containing `standard/`, `templates/`, `schema/` and no `tools/` mention, exactly the stale pattern `vlib_no_stale_payload` rejects. This is caught strictly on the segment's own content, not on any accidental property of a line count, a cap, or the surrounding numbered list (rev0's whole-paragraph-join rationale, rev1's cap-adjacent framing, and rev2's physical-line-boundary framing are all superseded by the format-agnostic redesign). This is the identical class of defect F-019 already fixed at three OTHER locations in this same file (Section 1 Core Directive, Section 2 item 2, Section 2.1 item 2, Section 5.1 -- per GIP.md's own 0.2.0 change_log entry) and the exact class the spec's sub-check (1) targets ('no stale 3-dir enumeration survives ANYWHERE') -- missed by F-019's own single-physical-line-scoped dry run because this instance wraps across two physical lines (`standard/`, `templates/`, or` / `schema/` no longer matches...`). Required fix, minimal and narrowly scoped (matching the F-026/026-precedent discipline for a pre-existing defect a new check's own non-breaking gate forces to the surface): reword the two-line span so the enumeration reads `standard/`, `templates/`, `schema/`, or `tools/` -- no other word in Section 4 changes. Verified by dry run (the REV3 normalize/segment pipeline reapplied to the corrected text, across BOTH the full default-mode corpus and the full payload-mode corpus): zero remaining sub-check-(1) failures; WITHOUT the fix, exactly this one failure in default mode, proving the fix is genuinely forced. Per NDS Sec 4 / validator C8, standard/GIP.md's frontmatter `version` bumps 0.2.0 -> 0.3.0 with one new `change_log` entry naming this exact fix.

files_modify.purpose: Add `check_c10_consistency` (signature: `check_c10_consistency <file> [<file> ...]`) implementing the three-sub-check design in design_notes.c10_subcheck_design_and_normalization_finding, composed from (sources, already sourced by C9) tools/verify_lib.sh's `vlib_no_stale_payload` for sub-check (1) -- no other vlib_* primitive is used (sub-checks 2/3 are C10-native line-number/anchor comparisons, not path resolution). Register C10 in main(): (a) DEFAULT branch -- immediately after the existing check_c9_path_resolution call (which already builds `c9_default_files`), add `check_c10_consistency "${c9_default_files[@]}"`, tallying passed/failed identically, so `SUMMARY: ${passed} passed, ${failed} failed` reads 10 on a clean tree. (b) --TARGET branch -- inside the existing `*.md)` case, after check_c9_path_resolution, add `check_c10_consistency "${target}"`. ADD a NEW `*.html)` case to the target switch (there is none today) that runs ONLY `check_c10_consistency "${target}"` (do not add C1/C2/C3/C9 for html targets -- see design_notes.three_mode_pinning). (c) --PAYLOAD branch -- after check_c9_path_resolution's payload-mode call, add `check_c10_consistency "${payload_md[@]}"`, so `SUMMARY (payload mode): ${passed} passed, ${failed} failed` reads 9 on a clean tree. Update print_usage(): add a `C10` paragraph to the 'Checks' block describing all three sub-checks (payload-set consistency via vlib_no_stale_payload composed over a FORMAT-AGNOSTIC normalize-then-segment pipeline of the body text -- strip HTML tags (replacing each with a space), collapse all whitespace/newlines to single spaces, then split into sentence segments on a `.`/`!`/`?` immediately followed by whitespace (never on `;`/`:`, never inside a `.md`/`.json`/`.sh` filename token); this is NOT any form of physical-line-based join (the whole-paragraph, capped, and cap-free-line-boundary designs were each tried and each rejected -- see design_notes.c10_subcheck_design_and_normalization_finding for the full history); discovery-order scoped to files containing BOTH the 'Bootstrapped-consumer discovery' and 'Framework-checkout fallback' canonical labels; framework-version anchor read from NIZAM.json per the External Anchor Rule); extend the 'Modes:' section to document the new `*.html` --target case and C10's presence in the default/--target/--payload sweeps. No change to any C1-C9 check function's own logic, to build_shipped_md_set/build_payload_md_set, to frontmatter_python, to check_c9_path_resolution, or to any existing die()/require_* call. Strictly additive to this file.

NDEBT-003(a) FIX (see design_notes.ndebt_003_decision; required for this feature's AT2 non-breaking gate -- the guide participates in C10's default-mode sweep as the version-anchor sub-check's only real target, and per methodology/02 Sec 10 discipline a shipped doc's own single-source-of-truth completeness gaps are exactly C10's domain): add one `methodology/05_eval_and_trace.md` entry to the methodology 'Key documents' list (currently `00_planning.md, 01_execution.md, 02_adversarial_tdd.md, 03_circuit_breaker.md, 04_tool_driven_state.md, 06_release_train.md`), in numeric position between `04_tool_driven_state.md` and `06_release_train.md`. No other content in that `

` element, and no other content anywhere else in the file, changes; the `` anchor and `` stay `0.5.1`, matching NIZAM.json unchanged (a separate, C10 sub-check (3) concern this edit does not touch). No frontmatter/version-bump/C8 obligation (unchanged from contract 026's reasoning: this file carries no YAML frontmatter and is not in the C1/C2/C3/C8 shipped-doc set).

NDEBT-003(b) FIX (see design_notes.ndebt_003_decision): correct the single bare, non-'/'-qualified, now-nonexistent filename token at Section 3.2's Minor-change example sentence from `05_release_train.md` to `06_release_train.md` (the file's real current name; `methodology/05_release_train.md` does not exist post the v0.5.x methodology renumbering that inserted `methodology/05_eval_and_trace.md`). No other prose in this file changes. Per NDS Sec 4 / validator C8, bump the frontmatter `version` from `0.2.0` to `0.3.0` and add exactly one new `change_log` entry (version 0.3.0, dated at implementation time) naming this exact fix.

NEW PRE-EXISTING-DEFECT FIX, discovered at this contract's authoring time, not previously logged in docs/planning/DEBT.md (see design_notes.pre_existing_finding_gip; required for this feature's AT2/AT3 non-breaking gate -- C10 sub-check (1), composed over the REV3 normalize/segment pipeline, non-vacuously fails on the CURRENT tree without this fix, confirmed by a full-corpus dry run in both default and payload modes): in Section 4 ('Drift Detection'), numbered item 3 ('Detecting local mutation'), add `tools/` to the enumerated list of injected directories subject to local-mutation drift detection -- the sentence currently spanning two physical lines ('If an injected file under `standard/`, `templates/`, or `schema/` no longer matches...') becomes 'If an injected file under `standard/`, `templates/`, `schema/`, or `tools/` no longer matches...'. No other word in Section 4, or anywhere else in the file, changes. Per NDS Sec 4 / validator C8, bump the frontmatter `version` from `0.2.0` to `0.3.0` and add exactly one new `change_log` entry (version 0.3.0, dated at implementation time) naming this exact fix, distinct from and in addition to the existing 0.2.0 (H5/H8) entry.

files_create.purpose: C10 sub-check (1) FAIL fixture (feature_list_004 F-027 acceptance test 4). A minimal markdown file (no YAML frontmatter, matching every existing C9/C10-class fixture's convention) whose entire body is a single, terminally-punctuated sentence naming `standard/`, `templates/`, and `schema/` together but omitting `tools/` -- verified by dry run (design_notes.c10_subcheck_design_and_normalization_finding) to be rejected by check_c10_consistency's normalize/segment vlib_no_stale_payload composition. Reachable only via `bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/stale_payload.md` (never in the default or payload sweep).

C10 sub-check (1) REGRESSION fixture closing the missing_acceptance_coverage gap from .agent/qa/027-contract-review.json (revision 0 rejection): a minimal markdown file (no frontmatter) whose body contains, in the SAME blank-line-delimited paragraph, TWO sentences: (a) a stale 3-directory enumeration ('standard/`, `templates/`, and `schema/` only') that itself wraps across two physical lines before its own terminal period, proving the normalize/segment pipeline still reconstructs (via whitespace-collapse) and catches a genuinely wrapped stale enumeration regardless of original line layout (missing_acceptance_coverage direction (a), true-positive preserved); and (b) an immediately-following, separately-terminally-punctuated, unrelated sentence that merely mentions `tools/` ('Elsewhere in this same paragraph, `tools/` hosts some unrelated utility scripts.'), proving sentence-segmentation on `[.!?]`+whitespace correctly splits it into its own segment and therefore does not swallow the violation (missing_acceptance_coverage direction (b), the exact false-pass the evaluator's oracle reproduced against rev0's whole-paragraph join). Under the REV3 format-agnostic redesign, whether the two sentences share one physical line or occupy separate ones is IMMATERIAL (unlike the rejected rev1/rev2 physical-line-based designs) -- segmentation depends only on `.`+whitespace, never on line boundaries; the fixture is kept in its original two-sentence, multi-line shape as historical/regression evidence of the class it closes. Verified by dry run: `[C10] FAIL` fires, tagged `payload-set`, naming only the first (stale) sentence's content -- the unrelated `tools/` sentence is never part of the reported offending text. Reachable only via --target.

C10 sub-check (1) REGRESSION fixture closing missing_acceptance_coverage from .agent/qa/027-contract-review.json (revision 1 rejection, issues[0]): a minimal markdown file (no frontmatter) containing a single genuine stale 3-directory enumeration sentence deliberately wrapped across MORE than 3 physical lines with NO terminal punctuation until the final line (e.g. 'The payload includes
the following:
`standard/`,
`templates/`,
and `schema/` only.' -- 5 physical lines, only the last ends in a period), proving the REV3 normalize/segment pipeline -- which collapses all whitespace/newlines before segmenting, so no physical line count is ever inspected -- still reconstructs the entire sentence up to its real terminal period and catches the violation, closing the exact false-negative class the evaluator's oracle proved against rev1's 3-line-capped design (reproduced there as `cap_fragment_falseneg.md`, where the cap force-closed the group before `schema/` was ever appended, causing a silent PASS). Verified by dry run: `[C10] FAIL` fires, tagged `payload-set`, naming the fully-reconstructed multi-line sentence. Reachable only via --target.

C10 sub-check (1) REGRESSION fixture closing missing_acceptance_coverage from .agent/qa/027-contract-review.json (revision 1 rejection, issues[1]): a minimal markdown file (no frontmatter) containing a single genuine stale 3-directory enumeration whose only physical-line break falls immediately after an internal `;` used as a list separator, not a sentence end (e.g. 'The payload includes `standard/`, `templates/`;\n`schema/` only.' -- 2 physical lines, the first ending in `;`), proving the REV3 normalize/segment pipeline -- whose sentence-segmentation regex `(?<=[.!?])\s+` structurally never matches on `;`/`:` (they are simply not in the character class, by construction, not by a tunable exception) -- does not prematurely segment at the semicolon and still reconstructs the full sentence up to its real terminal period, catching the violation and closing the exact false-negative class the evaluator's oracle proved against rev1's `;`/`:`-as-terminator rule (reproduced there as `midsentence_semicolon_falseneg.md`, where the `;` force-closed the group before `schema/` was ever appended, causing a silent PASS). Verified by dry run: `[C10] FAIL` fires, tagged `payload-set`, naming the fully-reconstructed sentence spanning both physical lines. Reachable only via --target.

C10 sub-check (1) REGRESSION fixture closing the missing_acceptance_coverage gap for the REV2 rejection (the HTML-`

`-collapsed false-pass that triggered this contract's REDESIGN mandate): a minimal, guide-styled standalone HTML file (reachable via the NEW `*.html)` --target case alongside tools/fixtures/version_drift.html, giving the html-target wiring a second, independent real-defect-class confirmation, not just the version-anchor sub-check) containing a stale `

`-wrapped 3-directory enumeration -- `

The payload as some people describe it lives under standard/, templates/, and schema/ only.

` -- immediately followed, on the SAME physical source line, with NO blank line and NO intervening whitespace beyond the tag boundary itself, by an unrelated `

`-wrapped sentence merely mentioning `tools/` -- `

Elsewhere on this page we separately note that tools/ hosts some unrelated utility scripts.

` -- reproducing exactly the shape a rev2 (physical-line-boundary) mechanism could never split (the sentence boundary falls at a `

` tag pair mid-physical-line, not at a physical line's end) but the REV3 redesign catches by construction: Stage 1 replaces each tag with a space, turning `only.

Elsewhere` into `only. Elsewhere`, exposing the `.`+whitespace boundary Stage 2's segmentation regex requires, independent of the original physical-line layout. Verified by dry run: `[C10] FAIL` fires, tagged `payload-set`, naming only the stale `

` sentence's content -- the unrelated `tools/` sentence is never part of the reported offending text. Reachable only via --target.

C10 sub-check (2) FAIL fixture (feature_list_004 F-027 acceptance test 5). A minimal markdown file (no frontmatter) containing BOTH canonical discovery-order labels in REVERSED order -- 'Framework-checkout fallback' (naming a bare repository-root `tools/skill.json` path) appearing before 'Bootstrapped-consumer discovery' (naming `.nizam/tools/skill.json`) -- verified by dry run to be rejected by check_c10_consistency's discovery-order sub-check (boot_line > fallback_line). Deliberately contains no shipped-extension-and-'/'-qualified unresolvable token (so it does not also, confusingly, fail C9) and no framework-version anchor pattern (so it does not also fail sub-check 3) -- isolates the FAIL to sub-check (2) alone. Reachable only via --target.

C10 sub-check (3) FAIL fixture (feature_list_004 F-027 acceptance test 6). A minimal standalone HTML file embedding `` and `0.4.0` -- a version deliberately different from NIZAM.json's `framework.version` (`0.5.1`) -- verified by dry run to be rejected by check_c10_consistency's version-anchor sub-check (External Anchor Rule: the expected value is read from NIZAM.json, not from this fixture). Contains no payload-enumeration or discovery-order-label content, isolating the FAIL to sub-check (3) alone. Reachable only via `bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/version_drift.html` -- the first C10/F-027 fixture requiring a NEW `*.html` case in the --target switch (see design_notes.three_mode_pinning).

non_goals: No fourth (or fifth) sub-check added to C10 beyond the spec's three (payload-set, discovery-order, version-anchor) -- see design_notes.no_fourth_subcheck_decision; NDEBT-003's 'guard against recurrence' clause is explicitly deferred as a new follow-up debt item (NDEBT-005), not silently mechanized here

No edit to any C1-C9 check function's logic, to check_c9_path_resolution itself, to frontmatter_python, to build_shipped_md_set/build_payload_md_set, or to any die()/require_* call in tools/validate.sh -- strictly additive (a new check function + 3 registration call-sites incl. one new `*.html` target case + print_usage documentation only)

No edit to tools/verify_lib.sh (F-023 is complete and read-only for this feature) -- C10's normalize/segment preprocessing, discovery-order label comparison, and version-anchor extraction all live in tools/validate.sh's check_c10_consistency, composing (never modifying) vlib_no_stale_payload
No C11 validator check -- that is F-028

No general doc-freshness or completeness pass on docs/guide/index.html, methodology/06_release_train.md, or standard/GIP.md beyond the single, precisely-described fix named in each file's scope entry above -- in particular, no other wording, section, or cross-reference in any of the three files is touched

No fix to tools/interface.md or docs/guide/index.html's Section 2/discovery-order prose beyond what contract 026 already landed -- this feature only ADDS the guide's missing methodology/05_eval_and_trace.md key-document entry, a distinct, unrelated location in the same file

No edit to NIZAM.json, CHANGELOG.md, CONTEXT.md, README.md, bootstrap.sh, schema/*.json, tools/interface.md, or any other standard/methodology/templates/registry/docs file beyond the three named exceptions above

No bump to NIZAM.json framework.version (stays 0.5.1) and no edit to docs/guide/index.html's `<meta name="framework-version">` anchor or footer version string -- that anchor's CURRENT value is the fixed point sub-check (3) verifies against, not something this feature changes

No edit to any immutable prior-phase/prior-feature artifact (completed .agent/contracts/*.json other than this feature's own 027.json, .agent/qa/*.json, .agent/evidence/*, .agent/run_state.json [orchestrator-owned], or any prior-phase feature list or product_spec)

No edit to docs/planning/DEBT.md (NDEBT-003's resolution and the new NDEBT-005 deferral are recorded by the orchestrator after this feature lands, per the WI-1/WI-2/contract-026 precedent -- DEBT.md is outside .agent and editing it here would trip the untracked-aware scope guard)
Does not release or tag anything; does not touch .github/workflows/

verification.acceptance_test: F-027 AT1: validate.sh registers a C10 check and print_usage documents it: grep -q 'C10' tools/validate.sh && bash tools/validate.sh --help | grep -q 'C10'
verification.command: f=tools/validate.sh; test -f "$f" || { echo "MISSING $f"; exit 1; }; grep -q 'check_c10_consistency' "$f" || { echo "C10 check function not found in tools/validate.sh"; exit 1; }; bash tools/validate.sh --help | grep -q 'C10' || { echo "--help does not document C10"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-027 AT2: the full sweep reports exactly ten checks passing on the CLEAN current tree (non-breaking, proven by the P2/P3 dry-runs + this contract's own normalize/segment dry-run): out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); echo "$out" | grep -qx 'SUMMARY: 10 passed, 0 failed'; echo "$out" | grep -q '[C10] PASS'
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; echo "$out" | tail -1 | grep -qx 'SUMMARY: 10 passed, 0 failed' || { echo "expected exact summary 'SUMMARY: 10 passed, 0 failed', got: $(echo "$out" | grep '^SUMMARY:')"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C10\] PASS' || { echo "no [C10] PASS line in output"; exit 1; }; test "$rc" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "tools/validate.sh exited $rc, expected 0"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-027 AT3: validate.sh exits 0 on the current tree: bash tools/validate.sh; test $? -eq 0
verification.command: bash tools/validate.sh >/dev/null 2>&1; rc=$?; test "$rc" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "tools/validate.sh exited $rc"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-027 AT4: C10 payload sub-check FAIL fixture: a tools/fixtures/ doc with a stale 3-dir payload enumeration (no tools/) is rejected: bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/stale_payload.md; test $? -ne 0
verification.command: test -f tools/fixtures/stale_payload.md || { echo "MISSING tools/fixtures/stale_payload.md"; exit 1; }; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/stale_payload.md 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "expected nonzero exit, got 0"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C10\] FAIL' || { echo "no [C10] FAIL line"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q 'payload-set' || { echo "[C10] FAIL output does not tag the payload-set sub-check"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-027 AT5: C10 discovery-order sub-check FAIL fixture: a tools/fixtures/ doc placing repo-root tools/skill.json before .nizam/tools/skill.json in a bootstrapped-consumer context is rejected: bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/bad_discovery_order.md; test $? -ne 0
verification.command: test -f tools/fixtures/bad_discovery_order.md || { echo "MISSING tools/fixtures/bad_discovery_order.md"; exit 1; }; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/bad_discovery_order.md 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "expected nonzero exit, got 0"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C10\] FAIL' || { echo "no [C10] FAIL line"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q 'discovery-order' || { echo "[C10] FAIL output does not tag the discovery-order sub-check"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-027 AT6: C10 version-anchor sub-check FAIL fixture: a fixture embedding a framework-version != NIZAM.json framework.version is rejected: bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/version_drift.html; test $? -ne 0
verification.command: test -f tools/fixtures/version_drift.html || { echo "MISSING tools/fixtures/version_drift.html"; exit 1; }; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/version_drift.html 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "expected nonzero exit, got 0"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C10\] FAIL' || { echo "no [C10] FAIL line"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q 'version-anchor' || { echo "[C10] FAIL output does not tag the version-anchor sub-check"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q '0\.4\.0' || { echo "[C10] FAIL output does not name the actual drifted value 0.4.0 -- content-blind stub suspected"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-027 AT7: C10 version anchor reads its expected value from NIZAM.json (External Anchor Rule), not from the doc under test: grep -q 'framework' tools/validate.sh && grep -q 'NIZAM.json' tools/validate.sh
verification.command: f=tools/validate.sh; grep -q "framework" "$f" || { echo "tools/validate.sh does not reference 'framework'"; exit 1; }; grep -q "NIZAM.json" "$f" || { echo "tools/validate.sh does not reference NIZAM.json"; exit 1; }; grep -Eq "json\.load\(open\('NIZAM\.json'\)\)\['framework'\]\['version'\]" "$f" || { echo "C10 does not read framework.version from NIZAM.json via python3"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-027 AT8: CONTEXT.md's legitimate framework-checkout discovery description is NOT false-flagged (regression proof P3): bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | grep -q '[C10] PASS'
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); echo "$out"; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C10\] PASS' || { echo "[C10] did not PASS on the default sweep -- CONTEXT.md or another file was false-flagged"; exit 1; }; grep -Eq 'NIZAM\.json.*first.*tools/skill\.json|resolves.*NIZAM\.json.*first' CONTEXT.md || true; grep -q 'bootstrapped-consumer discovery' CONTEXT.md && { echo "REGRESSION: CONTEXT.md now contains the canonical discovery-order label -- P3 scoping assumption invalidated, re-verify"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: F-027 AT9 (CORRECTED scope guard, see design_notes.ndebt_003_decision and design_notes.pre_existing_finding_gip -- the literal feature_list_004 wording only allow-lists tools/validate.sh + tools/fixtures/, but this contract's own dry-run proved 3 additional 1-line-class fixes to docs/guide/index.html, methodology/06_release_train.md, and standard/GIP.md are REQUIRED for AT2's non-breaking gate to hold; this entry asserts the corrected, actually-necessary scope via the untracked-aware vlib_scope_guard primitive, per methodology/02 Sec 10(b)): only tools/validate.sh, tools/fixtures/*, docs/guide/index.html, methodology/06_release_train.md, and standard/GIP.md changed outside .agent/
verification.command: source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'tools/validate.sh' 'tools/fixtures/' 'docs/guide/index.html' 'methodology/06_release_train.md' 'standard/GIP.md' && echo PASS || { echo "out-of-scope change(s) detected -- see vlib_scope_guard output above"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: F-027 AT10: no pre-existing check regressed (C1-C9 all PASS): for c in C1 C2 C3 C4 C5 C6 C7 C8 C9; do bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1 | grep -q "[$c] PASS" || exit 1; done
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); fail=0; for c in C1 C2 C3 C4 C5 C6 C7 C8 C9; do echo "$out" | grep -q "\[$c\] PASS" || { echo "regression: $c did not report PASS"; fail=1; }; done; test "$fail" -eq 0 && echo PASS || exit 1

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-cheat, methodology/02 Sec 10 discipline): the stale_payload.md FAIL is content-driven, not filename-keyed -- an exact copy under a neutral tmp filename, targeted directly, still reports [C10] FAIL payload-set
verification.command: test -f tools/fixtures/stale_payload.md || { echo "MISSING tools/fixtures/stale_payload.md"; exit 1; }; tmp=$(mktemp --suffix=.md); trap 'rm -f "$tmp"' EXIT; cp tools/fixtures/stale_payload.md "$tmp"; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target "$tmp" 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "neutral-filename copy unexpectedly PASSED -- suspected filename-keyed behavior"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q 'payload-set' || { echo "neutral-filename copy lost the payload-set FAIL tag"; exit 1; }; echo "PASS (correctly rejected, rc=$rc)"

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (missing_acceptance_coverage closure, .agent/qa/027-contract-review.json issues[0]/rev0 rejection): the normalize/segment pipeline BOTH still catches a genuinely wrapped stale enumeration (true-positive preserved) AND resists being fooled by an unrelated, same-paragraph sentence that merely mentions tools/ (the false-pass the evaluator's oracle reproduced against the original whole-paragraph-join design): bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/stale_payload_cosentence.md; test $? -ne 0
verification.command: test -f tools/fixtures/stale_payload_cosentence.md || { echo "MISSING tools/fixtures/stale_payload_cosentence.md"; exit 1; }; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/stale_payload_cosentence.md 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "expected nonzero exit -- the wrapped stale enumeration was NOT caught (false-pass regression), got 0"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C10\] FAIL' || { echo "no [C10] FAIL line"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q 'payload-set' || { echo "[C10] FAIL output does not tag the payload-set sub-check"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q 'only\.' || { echo "[C10] FAIL output does not name the wrapped stale sentence's own content -- true-positive-preservation not demonstrated"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q 'unrelated utility scripts' && { echo "[C10] FAIL output includes the UNRELATED tools/ sentence's text in the offending segment -- the normalize/segment pipeline merged them, the exact false-pass-adjacent defect this fixture exists to catch"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-cheat, methodology/02 Sec 10 discipline): the stale_payload_cosentence.md FAIL is content-driven, not filename-keyed -- an exact copy under a neutral tmp filename, targeted directly, still reports [C10] FAIL payload-set naming only the wrapped stale sentence, never the unrelated tools/ sentence
verification.command: test -f tools/fixtures/stale_payload_cosentence.md || { echo "MISSING tools/fixtures/stale_payload_cosentence.md"; exit 1; }; tmp=$(mktemp --suffix=.md); trap 'rm -f "$tmp"' EXIT; cp tools/fixtures/stale_payload_cosentence.md "$tmp"; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target "$tmp" 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "neutral-filename copy unexpectedly PASSED -- suspected filename-keyed behavior"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q 'payload-set' || { echo "neutral-filename copy lost the payload-set FAIL tag"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q 'unrelated utility scripts' && { echo "neutral-filename copy merged the unrelated tools/ sentence into the offending group"; exit 1; }; echo "PASS (correctly rejected, rc=$rc)"

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (missing_acceptance_coverage closure, .agent/qa/027-contract-review.json issues[0]/rev1 rejection): a genuine stale 3-directory enumeration wrapped across MORE THAN 3 physical lines with no interior terminal punctuation is still caught (proving the normalize/segment pipeline inspects no line count at all, unlike the rejected rev1 capped-join design): bash tools/validate.sh --target tools/fixtures/stale_payload_longwrap.md; test $? -ne 0

sentence into the offending segment"; exit 1; }; echo "PASS (correctly rejected, rc=$rc)"

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-cheat, methodology/02 Sec 10 discipline): the bad_discovery_order.md FAIL is content-driven, not filename-keyed -- an exact copy under a neutral tmp filename, targeted directly, still reports [C10] FAIL discovery-order
verification.command: test -f tools/fixtures/bad_discovery_order.md || { echo "MISSING tools/fixtures/bad_discovery_order.md"; exit 1; }; tmp=$(mktemp --suffix=.md); trap 'rm -f "$tmp"' EXIT; cp tools/fixtures/bad_discovery_order.md "$tmp"; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target "$tmp" 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "neutral-filename copy unexpectedly PASSED -- suspected filename-keyed behavior"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q 'discovery-order' || { echo "neutral-filename copy lost the discovery-order FAIL tag"; exit 1; }; echo "PASS (correctly rejected, rc=$rc)"

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (anti-cheat, methodology/02 Sec 10 discipline): the version_drift.html FAIL is content-driven, not filename-keyed -- an exact copy under a neutral tmp filename, targeted directly, still reports [C10] FAIL version-anchor and names 0.4.0
verification.command: test -f tools/fixtures/version_drift.html || { echo "MISSING tools/fixtures/version_drift.html"; exit 1; }; tmp=$(mktemp --suffix=.html); trap 'rm -f "$tmp"' EXIT; cp tools/fixtures/version_drift.html "$tmp"; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target "$tmp" 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -ne 0 || { echo "neutral-filename copy unexpectedly PASSED -- suspected filename-keyed behavior"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q 'version-anchor' && echo "$out" | grep -q '0\.4\.0' || { echo "neutral-filename copy lost the version-anchor FAIL tag or the offending value"; exit 1; }; echo "PASS (correctly rejected, rc=$rc)"

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (3-mode pinning proof, --payload): the payload sweep also PASSES C10 non-breaking, reporting the incremented payload-mode total (design_notes.three_mode_pinning)
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --payload 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -eq 0 || { echo "--payload exited $rc, expected 0"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C10\] PASS' || { echo "--payload mode did not report [C10] PASS"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | tail -1 | grep -qx 'SUMMARY (payload mode): 9 passed, 0 failed' || { echo "expected 'SUMMARY (payload mode): 9 passed, 0 failed', got: $(echo "$out" | grep '^SUMMARY')"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (3-mode pinning proof, new --target *.html case): the version_drift.html fixture is reachable ONLY through the new html target case, and a real, undrifted html-like file with the correct anchor would PASS C10 there (regression-shape proof using the real guide file itself as an html target)
verification.command: out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target docs/guide/index.html 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -eq 0 || { echo "targeting the real guide exited $rc, expected 0"; exit 1; }; echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C10\] PASS' || { echo "targeting docs/guide/index.html did not report [C10] PASS"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (NDEBT-003(a) remediation proof, docs/guide/index.html): the methodology key-documents list now names methodology/05_eval_and_trace.md alongside the pre-existing five entries, and no other content in the file changed besides this one addition
verification.command: f=docs/guide/index.html; grep -q 'methodology/05_eval_and_trace.md' "$f" || { echo "$f still missing the methodology/05_eval_and_trace.md key-document entry"; exit 1; }; for tok in methodology/00_planning.md methodology/01_execution.md methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md methodology/03_circuit_breaker.md methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md methodology/06_release_train.md; do grep -q "$tok" "$f" || { echo "$f lost pre-existing key-document entry $tok"; exit 1; }; done; grep -q 'name="framework-version" content="0.5.1"' "$f" || { echo "$f's framework-version meta anchor changed unexpectedly"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (NDEBT-003(b) remediation proof, methodology/06_release_train.md): the bare stale 05_release_train.md token is gone and replaced with 06_release_train.md, and the frontmatter version/change_log was bumped per C8
verification.command: f=methodology/06_release_train.md; grep -q '05_release_train.md' "$f" && { echo "$f still contains the stale bare 05_release_train.md token"; exit 1; }; grep -q '06_release_train.md' "$f" || { echo "$f is missing the corrected 06_release_train.md token"; exit 1; }; source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_version_increased "$f" || { echo "$f's frontmatter version did not strictly increase vs HEAD"; exit 1; }; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target "$f" 2>&1); rc=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc" -eq 0 && echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C1\] PASS' && echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C2\] PASS' && echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C3\] PASS' && echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C9\] PASS' && echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C10\] PASS' || { echo "$f failed the --target sweep"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (pre-existing-finding-GIP remediation proof, standard/GIP.md): Section 4 item 3 now names tools/ alongside standard/, templates/, schema/, the frontmatter version/change_log was bumped per C8, and the file still passes its own --target sweep
verification.command:
f=standard/GIP.md; python3 - "$f" <<'PY'
import re, sys
text = open(sys.argv[1], encoding='utf-8').read()
m = re.search(r'Detecting local mutation.*?no longer matches', text, re.S)
if not m:
    print('could not locate the Detecting local mutation sentence'); sys.exit(1)
span = m.group(0)
for tok in ('standard/', 'templates/', 'schema/', 'tools/'):
    if tok not in span:
        print(f'Detecting local mutation span still missing {tok}'); sys.exit(1)
sys.exit(0)
PY
rc=$?; test "$rc" -eq 0 || { echo "GIP.md Section 4 item 3 fix not found/incomplete"; exit 1; }; source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_version_increased "$f" || { echo "$f's frontmatter version did not strictly increase vs HEAD"; exit 1; }; out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --target "$f" 2>&1); rc2=$?; echo "$out"; test "$rc2" -eq 0 && echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C1\] PASS' && echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C2\] PASS' && echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C3\] PASS' && echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C9\] PASS' && echo "$out" | grep -q '\[C10\] PASS' || { echo "$f failed the --target sweep"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

verification.acceptance_test: ADDITIONAL (non-goal enforcement): NIZAM.json framework.version is unchanged, and no schema/*.json, CHANGELOG.md, CONTEXT.md, README.md, bootstrap.sh, tools/interface.md, or .github/workflows/ file was touched
verification.command: v=$(python3 -c "import json; print(json.load(open('NIZAM.json'))['framework']['version'])"); test "$v" = "0.5.1" || { echo "NIZAM.json framework.version changed to $v, expected 0.5.1 unchanged"; exit 1; }; git diff --quiet HEAD -- NIZAM.json CHANGELOG.md CONTEXT.md README.md bootstrap.sh tools/interface.md schema .github/workflows || { echo "an out-of-scope file was modified"; exit 1; }; echo PASS

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/027-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/027-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/027-verify-27.txt for the twenty-seventh). Every evidence file MUST comply with methodology/04_tool_driven_state.md Sec 5's Evidence Capture Convention: the exact invocation as the FIRST line, captured output next, a literal EXIT:<code> as the LAST line, replayable from the repository root, with NO session-absolute paths. One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
